errors.message: cannot find type SslStream - type not defined when no SSL features enabled, but cfg attributes may not be working correctly with Verus

events.reason: Fix SslStream type not found error - cfg attributes may not work properly inside verus! blocks. Need to ensure imports/types are properly conditional or provide stub definition.
events.error_targeted: cannot find type SslStream in util/refined_tcp_stream.rs:26

events.reason: Fixed SslStream type not found error: moved conditional pub(crate) use statements for SslStream and SslContextImpl OUTSIDE the verus! {} block in ssl.rs. This allows Rust's cfg processor to handle conditional compilation properly. When no SSL features are enabled, the type imports don't exist, but that's fine because the Stream enum variants using them are also behind the same cfg guards.

events.reason: Convert 3 unsupported iterator patterns to Verus-compatible loops: (1) mpsc::Iter in response.rs, (2) str::Split in util/mod.rs, (3) ClientConnection iterator in lib.rs
events.error_targeted: 8 unsupported iterator errors (3 distinct patterns)

events.reason: Converted 3 unsupported iterator patterns: (1) mpsc::Receiver.iter() → loop with try_recv() in response.rs, (2) str::Split → loop with .next() in util/mod.rs, (3) ClientConnection iterator → loop with .next() in lib.rs (2 occurrences). All changes compile with cargo build and preserve semantics.

Fixed &mut types error in client.rs:91: replaced .by_ref().bytes().next() with direct Read::read() call using single-byte buffer. Eliminates mutable reference pattern unsupported by Verus while preserving exact semantics (reads one byte at a time, handles EOF/errors identically). Cargo build and all tests pass.

events.trigger: The verifier does not yet support the following Rust feature: &mut types with .by_ref().bytes().next()

errors.message: unsizing operation from &mut [u8; 1] to &mut [u8] not supported - idiom-converter fix introduced new error

events.reason: idiom-converter fix introduced new blocking error: Verus doesn't support unsizing &mut [u8; 1] to &mut [u8]. Need alternative approach.
events.error_targeted: unsizing operation from &mut [u8; 1] to &mut [u8] in client.rs:92

events.reason: Fixed unsizing operation error in client.rs:92: changed `self.next_header_source.read(&mut single_byte_buf)` to `self.next_header_source.read(single_byte_buf.as_mut_slice())`. This explicitly converts the &mut [u8; 1] array reference to &mut [u8] slice reference, avoiding the unsizing coercion that Verus doesn't support. Semantics preserved perfectly - still reads one byte at a time.

errors.message: &mut types error persists - as_mut_slice() still requires &mut. I/O operations fundamentally need &mut which Verus doesn't support.

events.reason: Mark read_next_line function as external - it performs I/O operations requiring &mut types that Verus fundamentally doesn't support. This is HTTP header parsing, not core business logic.
events.error_targeted: &mut types error in read_next_line I/O function

events.reason: Marked read_next_line function as external with #[verifier::external] attribute. This function performs I/O operations (reading from network stream byte-by-byte) that require &mut types which Verus fundamentally doesn't support. It's HTTP protocol parsing, not core business logic. Added VERUS-EXTERNAL comment explaining why.

errors.message: complex arguments to &mut parameters - headers.iter() call itself is the problem

events.reason: Extract .iter() call to separate variable - Verus doesn't like ANY method chain starting with .iter() as argument
events.error_targeted: complex arguments to &mut parameters at headers.iter()

events.reason: Extracted headers.iter() to separate mutable variable: let mut headers_iter = headers.iter(); let connection_header = headers_iter.find(...). The .find() method requires &mut self on the iterator, and Verus does not support complex expressions (like method calls) as arguments to &mut parameters. By extracting to a variable, the mutable borrow is explicit and simple. cargo build and all lib tests pass.

events.trigger: complex arguments to &mut parameters are currently unsupported
